title: abies religiosa subsp. perotensis
intro: Abies religiosa subsp. perotensis, also known as Mexican fir, is a subspecies of evergreen fir tree native to Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oils, which have been found to exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Mexican fir include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, essential oils, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
